articles.title: Partnerships & Stakeholders: Science and Technology Community
articles.description: The role of global science, academia, research and innovation is enshrined in the Sendai Framework for Disaster Risk Reduction where it is recognized for the catalytic, boundary-pushing and transformative potential it holds for addressing the dynamic drivers of disaster risk.

articles.title: Learn, Connect, Share: Exploring resources for students in disaster resilience
articles.description: Are you a student passionate about building a safer, more resilient world? Join us for a live introduction to PreventionWeb, UNDRR’s global platform for disaster risk reduction (DRR) knowledge.

articles.title: Higher Council for Civil Defense (HCfCD)
articles.description: The Higher Council of Civil Defence (HCCD) is the main Palestinian decision-making body on disaster risk management.

articles.title: Global Tsunami Model Association (GTM)
articles.description: The Global Tsunami Model (GTM) Network aims to save lives, reduce losses, and enhance resilience by advancing tsunami science, providing expert information, and promoting dialogue about tsunami hazards and risks.

articles.title: Emergencia Animal
articles.description: Emergencia Animal's mission is to integrate animal welfare into disaster risk reduction frameworks across Spain, ensuring that families, animal shelters, and communities can effectively prepare for, respond to, and recover from emergencies.

articles.title: São Tomé and Príncipe transforms their EW4All commitments into action
articles.description: In March 2026, São Tomé and Príncipe endorsed its EW4All roadmap to establish inclusive early warning systems. The roadmap benefited from the high-level political support and government ownership, paving the way to effectiveness and sustainability.

articles.title: From governance reform to early action: expanding early warning systems in the DRC
articles.description: The Government of the Democratic Republic of Congo hosted a national workshop on the Early Warnings for All (EW4All) initiative from 28 to 29 January 2026. The workshop built upon two landmark DRM decrees the DRC adopted in 2025.
